pitch_rationale: Area-weighted across 20 roof segments from Google Solar API (imageryQuality=HIGH): 38.93° → 10:12. Solar's underlying DSM model reports ~5° MAE in published benchmarks (Satellite Sunroof, NeurIPS 2024).
footprint_rationale: The orange bounding box spans approximately 228 × 332 px (48.3 × 70.4 ft), giving a bounding area of ~3,400 sq ft. The subject home is a complex multi-section hip roof; the upper ~85% of the bounding box is covered by interconnected hip roof sections, while the lower ~15% is open driveway/yard. A fill factor of ~78% was applied, yielding a footprint of ~2,650 sq ft. Ridge lines are short (3–4 horizontal peaks across sub-masses). Hip lines are numerous (~10 runs) as the dominant roof style. Valleys are visible as dark inward-angle lines where sub-roofs intersect (~4–5 valleys). Rake edges are minimal since this is primarily a hip-style roof. Eave lines wrap the full outer perimeter of each hip section. Confidence is moderate due to roof complexity and partial overlap with adjacent structures near the left edge of the bounding box.

condition.rationale: The subject home at 5914 Copper Lilly Lane features a complex multi-gable/hip roof clad in dark charcoal asphalt shingles. Across all visible roof planes — including the upper northern section, central intersecting gables, and lower southern slopes — the shingle color and texture are consistent and uniform with no discernible color breaks, patching, or mismatched material. Ridge lines are geometrically straight with no visible sag or deflection. There are no tarps, coverings, or large debris present. No green/dark biological growth patches are visible on any slope. No areas of exposed decking or obvious missing material are present. No rust streaking or water-staining tracks are observable. Image resolution and lighting are sufficient for a reliable surface-level assessment. No findings meet the confidence threshold for reporting.

fence_reason: vision roof area 3450 differs from solar 4369 by 21.0% (>12%)
